synthesis.summary: DAL is up ~1.1% today, continuing an 8.6% five-day run that likely reflects pre-earnings positioning as analysts revise forecasts ahead of the upcoming earnings call. The single available news item covers analyst estimate revisions but provides no specific detail on direction or magnitude of those changes, so it explains the sentiment context but not the full multi-day momentum. RSI at 76 flags the stock as overbought, and today's below-average volume (rel. volume 0.45) suggests this is drift rather than a fresh catalyst-driven surge.

Verizon was removed from the Dow Jones Industrial Average and replaced by Alphabet, triggering forced selling from index-tracking funds that must rebalance out of VZ. The index removal is a structural negative — passive fund outflows can persist until the rebalancing is complete — which explains the elevated volume (2x normal) and the sharp 3.2% single-day drop.

MSTR is down 6.77% over the past 5 days (-16.83%), tracking a broader Bitcoin-related selloff. A news item referencing the abandonment of a "Never Sell Bitcoin" approach signals a potential shift in the crypto holding strategy that underpins MSTR's core valuation thesis, but the analyst note alone doesn't fully account for the magnitude of the multi-day decline — broader BTC price weakness and macro sentiment (Warsh speech, jobs data) are likely compounding the move.

Joby and Toyota announced a new manufacturing joint venture, deepening their existing air taxi partnership. This shifts the relationship from a financial/development backing to a formal production-stage commitment, which materially de-risks Joby's path to commercialization. The 5% move reflects the structural significance of Toyota bringing its manufacturing expertise into a JV, not just capital.
